definition: A system component responsible for one stage of the workflow — planner, dev, reviewer, qa-* — with scoped, non-overlapping responsibilities.
Root document that defines the repository's operating model. It is the highest source of truth; any conflict is resolved in its favor.
Organizational model that divides the repository into: Governance → Conceptual → Runtime → Product → Validation, ensuring traceability from rule to code.
List of commands or steps executed to confirm that a change did not break anything before marking it as complete.
Layer that models the roles, responsibilities, and protocols of each agent. The .agent.md and SKILL.md files live here.
Percentage of features, flows, and error cases covered by automated tests. A gap in coverage is a risk.
Boundary or uncommon case that can break assumptions in the main flow. Always defined before coding begins.
Safety rule or control that reduces execution risks. Can be an instruction in AGENTS.md or a restriction in a .toml file.
Expected flow when everything works correctly without errors. The baseline for minimum QA coverage.
Practice of designing an application to support multiple languages without rewriting code. This course supports ES/EN.
Property of an operation that produces the same result regardless of how many times it runs. Critical for CI scripts and configuration.
Special agent (meta) that evaluates whether work is mature enough to advance. It does not make changes; it only judges quality and readiness.
First agent in the workflow. It clarifies the task, identifies risks, and breaks down the work before any code is touched.
Instruction you give to the agent to guide a concrete task. A good prompt is explicit, scoped, and backed by verifiable context.
Set of practices to ensure software works as expected. Includes strategy, execution, regression, and reporting.
New failure in existing behavior caused by recent changes. Detected with automated regression tests.
Agent that reviews already-implemented code without modifying it. Detects bugs, edge cases, and maintainability issues.
Strategy to revert changes when an implementation fails. Must be planned before making changes, not after.
Layer that configures how agents are executed in practice. The .toml files define available tools, constraints, and permissions.
File that defines the specialized capabilities of an agent: domain knowledge, instructions, and refined workflows.
Document or artifact with official priority when information conflicts. Hierarchy: AGENTS.md > .agents/*.md > .codex/*.toml.
Organized collection of tests covering units, integration, and end-to-end behavior of the system.
Human-readable configuration format used in .codex/agents/*.toml to define tools, constraints, and agent behavior.
Ability to follow the origin of a decision or change from requirement to code. Central principle of the workflow.
Official sequence of stages — planner → qa-strategy → dev → qa → reviewer → qa-regression — to build software with clarity and less rework.
